In between the bases, there is a run down five-floor building and a few relatively intact ones. If the shit hits the fan, you can sit it out there. There are also some garages and a couple of small houses around that area. There are a couple of five-floor buildings and a dozen garages on the bandits' side too. The former city council building is on the other side of the square.
A truly nasty guy named Dushman. I've seen him carrying around a folder with some documents a couple of times. I reckon there's something interesting inside.
Strelok! Where the hell have you been??? I haven't seen you or the guys since the last raid! I was getting worried!
So, you feeling better? You thinking clearly?

Yeah, you're Strelok! Who else would you be? I'm Doc, don't you recognize me? What happened to you, friend? You don't look well. We went into the Zone together for 2 years. You really don't remember me?
You don't??? Strelok, what the...? You're not joking around, are you? Man... What about the guys, our guys? Fang, Ghost? You remember them, don't you?
I don't remember Fang, and Ghost is dead - I found his body in Yantar.
Ghost!!?!?! Dead??!!! Dammit, how could that happen?! They... they were the best. What happened to you?
I don't know what happened, I don't remember.

Wait, I need to calm down. That's some shitty news man. I think it's all linked with your last trip into the Zone. When we saw each other the last time, you left me the key from the stash at Prypiat and the map of how to find it. I've managed to keep it - hid it from the Mercs. Here, take it, hide it well. Perhaps you'll find somthing there. You were saying that there's some trick with the Monolith. But I don't understand what it was about, and you didn't either.
I see. Where are we? I don't remember how I got here.
We're in Dead City! In the Merc camp.
We're in Dead City!.. In the Merc camp...
By the way, I have information about the Mercs. Interested?
I have a map which details the location of their base, guard posts, the way to get in without being noticed and a lot more.
Sounds valuable. I'll give you 3000 for it.
Great! Here's your reward. Soon stalkers will argue about whether the Mercs even existed at all!
As you wish, I'm not giving you any more.
Nothing worthwhile. Just the usual gossip. Sorry.
If you find out something more specific come back. I'll pay you.
This area is off limits, stalker.
But I need to go there.
Tough luck. Unless you REALLY need to go, then the price is 500.
Now listen up. We'll pretend for a moment it's nothing but mosquitoes and butterflies around here. What happens beyond this point is your own damn business.
Alright, keep moving! Get your ass outta here, before we change our mind.
I don't have that kind of money.
Then go back where you came from and get it. And don't you try and sneak past us, you hear? Trust me, it's not worth dying for.
Get out of here, Marked One. Walk away or crawl away, the choice is yours.
Our boss here wanted to see you. But that doesn't make you a welcome guest. Keep moving.
Guide, I need to see Doctor.
Well, look at that. How do you know about Doctor?
Ghost sent me. Help me get in touch with Doctor. It's urgent.
Well, why didn't you say so! Doctor is in Strelok's hiding place. If you are who you say you are, you probably know where it is.
Could you tell me more about this place?

This is the road to the Zone. If I were you, I wouldn't get too close to the army outpost or let those soldiers see you - they've got orders to shoot on sight. Further up North there's a broken bridge, but they've set up an outpost there too. Open areas are crawling with wild dogs, so it's not a safe place for rookies. Oh, and a pack of mutants shows up every now and then.
What about the soldiers at the bridge? I've got to get past them somehow.

Alright, you've got two options. If Kuznetsov is on duty, keep in mind that he is quite fond of money. Good chance he'll let you through for a bribe. But if you're low on cash or if it's someone else's shift, there is a different way. Go on the left side of the bridge, along the railway. Just make sure they don't see you! You'll come across a small tunnel that goes through the embankment. And another thing: you might also land a date with some zombies down there.
What's further up North beyond the army outpost?
The Garbage, that’s what. Now there's a depressing place. So much contaminated shit in some spots that swimming would be easier than walking.
Hello. Wolf has already sent word of you. You got any questions?
Yes. How many bandits in the camp?

Seven or eight people. Two by the gate, two more in the building to the right near the fire, and another two in the one to the left - that's where they're keeping Nimble. Someone usually hangs around the yard as well. That's about it. Shall we say a prayer and go?
Let's go. Round up your men.
No. We'd make too much noise. I'll go on my own.
Well, do as you please. Shout if something is up... To hell with you, Rambo wannabe!
We went hunting and hunt we did. I bet Wolf will be pleased.
There's more of 'em! Find the bastards and take them down!
Whoa, man! Now that was something! You wiped them out and left nothing for us. I'd never believe it if I hadn't seen it with my own eyes! That blew me away, man...
So, where are the piles of enemies you killed? You talk the talk, but you don't walk the walk. Get lost!
Sorry, brother, but could go somewhere else? I'm here to meet someone...
I've got a couple of questions.
What can you tell me about the Zone?

That's all you wanna know? Take a week off and then come back to see me. Look, my time ain't cheap and I'm kinda busy right now. Here are the basics and then you're on your own. About three kilometers North of here, you'll find the Garbage. That's the place they used to dump all the radioactive waste from the station. High radiation levels, anomalies all over the place, so there are loads of artifacts to be found there... but not very valuable. Just beware of the gangs and don't cross the Duty patrols. West of the Garbage is the former Ahroprom Research Institute. Little in terms of artifacts there so you can skip it. And to the East, there's the Dark Valley. Who knows... That's a pretty weird place. It seems quiet enough, apart from a bunch of gangsters roaming around. But I got a feeling there's more to it than meets the eye. Oh, and there's the Rostok factory right beyond the Garbage. That's stalker territory. You'll find lots of clued-up folks there, they'll tell you the rest.
Thanks. OK, now I'm listening very carefully.
Trader told me you might know something about Strelok.

Why is everyone taking such a keen interest in him lately? OK, I can't say I know him personally. We did "communicate" though: my guys took some shots at him once. No one got hurt - it was clear his party had been waiting for someone else, so we parted in peace. Cussed each other out across a ravine and went on our separate ways. Siryi, my brother, is now in the hangar at the Garbage - I'll dish you his coordinates so you two can have a chat. He knows more about Strelok. Be careful at the exit, a small gang has settled down in those parts. By the way, I got a few monsters chasing me and it doesn't look like they've given up yet. You mind helping me deal with them? It's a walk in the park really.
Thanks for the info, I appreciate it.
Hey, man, you saved me twice, and that's not something I forget. Take this... that's all I got!
Our boss here wanted to see you. But that doesn't make you a welcomed guest. Keep moving.
Wait, Marked One! No one at the camp is gonna to talk to you right now.
What kind of idiot would chat with a former passenger of the "death truck"? First, you gotta prove you're OK to deal with.
What "death truck?" What are you talking about?

Ah, hell only knows what it really is. But whatever it is, it's bad, about as bad as it gets... Why am I talking to you about this anyway! Maybe I'm just superstitious, but you, Marked One, should really take a hike before something happens to you.
What can I do to make people believe me?
Pass Trader's test while we watch. If you're alright - if you're not trigger-happy and you use your head - then we'll definitely find some common ground. Don't you worry.
Hello, Marked One. What do you want?
Listen up, Marked One, you will come in handy! We could really use some help.

I'm Fanatic, and I'm filling in for the leader since Wolf left. A few Mercs came by here yesterday... and they were some tough bastards, let me tell you. They come here and start making serious demands - "You've got one day to clear this camp out. It's gonna be our new base." Tonight they're coming back, and as you can imagine, they won't be in the mood for a chat.
So, what are you going to do?

We're gonna set up an ambush 'cause a direct attack would probably be the end of us. They've got the fighting gear and everything, plus they're trained in tactics like you can't even imagine. And all we've got here are little boys. So, we've decided to just sit tight in these houses and make it look like we're gone. Then when the right moment comes, we attack. Fighters like you are just the thing we could use right now. And we'd make sure to thank you properly after it's all over. You know we're good for it! So, what do you say, will you help us out?

Let's spend the rest of the time preparing for the fight. Here's the plan: I'll put the most hopeless rookies in houses and basements and tell them not to make a move - otherwise they might just slaughter our own troops. You and me and a couple other decent shots will take the best positions to have an eye on the area around the fire. As soon as they come close to the fire, we let the bullets fly from everywhere! They'll be coming from the bridge... and I don't think we'll have to wait long. So, find yourself a hiding place with a view, then wait for my command. Take a look around, but remember that your odds aren't as good in the open. It's your call.
That's a shame. We could really use a fighter like you. I guess we'll have to do it on our own...
Waiting for these boy scouts. I got a feeling they're just around the corner.
You're a real killer, Marked One! I wouldn't want to stand in your way. Ever!
Here, I hope you won't forget about us. And by the way... a million thanks for the help. We saved the young ones, Marked One.
Hey, Marked One, great to see you!
By the way, where did Wolf go?

Oh, he got a message from a stalker who was passing by. He packed his things, grabbed a box of dynamite and went deep in the Zone. I'm still getting back in shape after the wounds I got at the Cordon so he asked me to look after the kids.
Go ahead, I'll try to help.
What do you know about the Zone?
I know many things. Be more specific.
What's in the center of the Zone?

Ahem, that's a question alright... I've been looking for the answer for two years! I still haven't found a way to the center, at least not one I could take and come back alive. My friends and I tried to break through a couple of times. I barely made it back, but my friends - well, some did, some didn't... You'll ask why we went there at all. We were chasing our dreams, trying to make them come true.

There is this beautiful story about a Wish-Granting Machine. They say there is a spot in the center of the Zone where any desires can come true, no matter how bizarre. But you know what? I know for a fact there's lots of ways to kick the bucket in the center of the Zone, and I don't believe in the Wish-Granting Machine anymore. Don't take that risk. I wouldn't.
How did the Zone come to be?
There was another explosion here in 2006. That's how it came about. I know that's not a real answer. Very few know the whole truth, if anyone at all.
What brought you to the Zone?

I used to work in a mine before they closed it down. I had no money and nothing to look forward to. Then I heard some rumors about the fabulous riches hidden inside the Zone. I had some experience under my belt, so I thought "What the hell?". Can't say I've become rich, but some cash came my way, that's for sure. This is a typical story; most of us got here the same way.
What's the most dangerous thing in the Zone?

Hard to say. There are so many dangers and surprises, you just can't run into all of them. There's more than enough for one person anyway. I got one toe bitten off, my hair is falling out from the radiation, and half my leg got fried in a Scorcher... Things there are real bad. Another time I happened to come face to face with a bloodsucker - there's a meeting I'd like to forget. So, we can talk all you like, but until you''ll never kow until you experience it on your own. The only advice I can give is beware - everywhere and always.
Have you heard about a guy called Strelok?

I seem to remember he's one of the tough ones. Never actually met the man. Well, I haven't come close to the center in a while and all we get here is rookies. You should find someone who hangs out deep in the Zone and have a chat with him.
What do you know about "death trucks?"

I know nothing and that's fine by me, thank you! Sorry, I know about your situation. Too much mysticism about it. You spend a month in these parts and you can't help but become superstitious about life. Dead people in the trunk, dead people behind the wheel... Who are they, where did they come from and why? I can only hope they didn't rise up from the grave.
Hi. I need to see Nimble. Would you know where I could find him?

Nimble got a raw deal. His group was attacked by some bandits a little ways from here. All he could do was send me an SOS message. It looked like his pals went under. My guys told me these bastards are now at the old car park, the one across the road.
You don't rescue your own people from prison? Not what I would call friendly. Or is it that you're just too weak to do it?

I'd bop you one, but what's the use? It's not that simple. I got too few people, and even those I got are mostly rookies. I can't take that risk. If we lose this camp, things will get even worse for all the stalkers out there. And what about yourself? If you aren't too scared, I won't let my pride make me say no to a helping hand. So, will you help me deal with these thugs?
Think I could do it alone?
Nah, you stand no chance on your own. My scouts are good soldiers and right now they're right behind those freaks. Together you can make a run for it. What do you think? You got the guts?
Way to go! Alright, I gave you the guys' coordinates. I'll let them know about you right now.
I got the guts, but I won't go.
Yeah, like hell you do. Fine, get lost!
Your guys and I made it a-ok at the car park.
That's awesome, man! Here, take this. That's my personal reward for you.
We went over the car park with a fine-toothed comb. It's all clear now.
Wow! My men tell me you gave them a real beating! Here, take this money... Just a memento, not counting our huge gratitude. Plus, I got something else for you here...
I can't waste time on idle chit-chat. Take a hike.
I got that, I'm not stupid.
Hello, Marked One. What's the occasion?
What can I tell you. There were bandits at the car park, so we did some cleaning up. That's about it.
You aren't the bragging kind, are you! Look, since you've dealt with these thugs, I can't just let you go like that...
Thanks, brother! You did us a great service! I don't even know how to thank you...
Listen, I need that flash drive you were taking to the trader. Do you have it?
Yeah, I got it... Those morons can't even do a proper search. Well, what the heck, take it! After all, you saved my life. It was well hidden, but these thugs can't frisk for shit.
Thanks. Where did you keep it anyway? Who knows, might come in handy.

I didn't keep it down THERE so don't worry. I've worked for Trader for a long time so I can store information safely! By the way, I told the thugs a nice little story about a cache full of artifacts and they ordered one guy to go there. The cache exists, but it's only got one artifact. I can give you its coordinates if you want.
But don't forget: there's lots of wild animals there. Besides, the gangster they sent is armed with more than a scoop and a shovel, so be careful. Thanks, man.
Mission complete. Here's the box from the army outpost.

Good! You were a great help. But, of course a "thank you" won't buy you a piece of bread. Here's three... wait, two thousand pieces of eight and we're square! By the way, the soldiers will certainly notice something missing, so things are going to heat up. You better get going, and the faster, the better.
Got any special missions for me?
What special missions? You haven't even finished the last one!

Well, here's the deal... A box containing the reports made by an expedition that went deep North into the Zone has been taken to the army outpost. By day, it's guarded as if it was filled with pure plutonium, but for some reason at night they only leave two guards.
Sneak into the outpost quietly and "recover" the box. Do that and I'll pay you anything...within reason. Will you do it?
I'll get the cash ready - you haven't let me down once. Just one other thing... keep it low profile. If the military gets pissed off we're all in for a lot of trouble.
No, I can't take it on yet.
C'mon man, it's not as bad as it sounds. A bunch of drunk recruits green as grass with a single NCO...Well, OK, but I was really counting on you, Marked One...
Listen, Marked One... I've done some thinking, and here's what I'd like to offer... Generally speaking, what you really need is to find Strelok. Right?

Anyway, here's the thing about Strelok. There's a stalker that goes by this name. They say he's found a path to the northern reaches of the Zone. And that is a virgin area, a real goldmine for artifacts... Yeah... Anyway, I can help you find him... However... As you can imagine, it won't be a free ride. You'll have to work it off... But in the long run we will both benefit: You're going to waste that Strelok fella and find out what's happened to you... And I'll find... well, I mean... I'll get my share of our mutual profit, he-he... So what do you say? Deal?

Alright, listen... You mean business, I can see that... And I... I mean, we need guys like you. We're doing sort of an investigation with some other traders. We want to open up the path to the North, to the center of the Zone. And near the center someone or something is trying to hamper our progress... How the heck this Strelok managed to sneak through, I don't know... You see, there's this one area where your brain starts boiling. An eerie place... Anyway, to cut a long story short - a couple of guys have been spying on them grunts for me, not far from here. Apparently, the ones at the Ahroprom Research Institute, have dug up something. Something major. I'm positive it has something to do with the center of the Zone. Anyway, whatever they've dug up, it's secured somewhere on the third floor of the Institute. And we really need this stuff. You see what I mean?
Once you have the briefcase, don't bring it here. Take it straight to the Barman. He runs the stalker bar, the 100 Rads, I'll upload the coordinates and all the data you need to your PDA. Clear?

You'll have to go North through the Garbage, then turn West. You'll end up at the Ahroprom Research Institute after a few kilometers. And be careful, the radiation level is way too high at the Garbage. So I suggest you cough up for some antirad or vodka. Well, good luck.

Got a job for you, Marked One... I want you to find a stalker called Nimble, he was carrying some very important information. He disappeared somewhere near the bridge. Find him, dead or alive - I don't care, I need the flash drive with the info. Visit Wolf from the local camp, and ask him. He certainly knows where that guy can be.
Marked One, what are you doing here? I told you to find Nimble!
So, you paid X18 a visit? Got the documents?

Well, well... I see... Now that's interesting, very interesting! If I told anyone they wouldn't believe me! Hmm... Yeah... Oh! Would you just look at that! I didn't expect this... it's pretty bad, eh... Well, here take these back and give them to the Barkeep. Oh, and I owe you for your efforts.

Wait. Here, take this pistol and ammo. You won't last two seconds in the Zone without a weapon! By the way, when you were brought here, you had a knife. I took it off you just in case your head wasn't quite working. You look OK though, so here it is.
So Marked One, any progress on our mutual business?
The choice is yours. Either I brainwash you like I usually do with all the rookies or I treat you like a real stalker and give you a mission straight away.
Hey, Marked One! Good timing, I've got a little job for you.
Treat me like a rookie, I don't remember shit.

Got a job for you, Marked One... I want you to find a stalker called Nimble, he was carrying some very important information. He disappeared somewhere near the bridge. Find him, dead or alive - I don't care, I need the flash drive with the info. Visit Wolf from the local camp, and ask him. He certainly knows where that guy can be.
That's all for now. Bring me the flash drive and we'll consider the fact that you've partially paid me for saving you.
Hey, Marked One. Glad to see you alive.
Greetings, Marked One! Did you manage?
Great, Marked One. One more little favor and we're even.
Where's the flash drive with the info?
That's a pity, Marked One, a real pity. We can't talk business until you bring it to me...
Good...We'll consider that you passed the test. I'd suggest you avoid going to the rookie camp.
So, did you bring the artifact?
Well, until you bring it, we've got nothing to talk about. Got it?
Marked One, I've told you everything already. Get outta here!
I need answers to a few questions.
Ask away but I don’t know everything. I sit here all day while life goes on outside, in the Zone. I can tell you about the Zone in general and a little about the nearby areas where I've been myself.
Tell me about the Zone in general.
What does the army do in the Zone?

They guard and protect, what else? No one really knows whether they're guarding the Zone or protecting the rest of the world from it. Sometimes they send whole expeditions here to look for something... But I'll tell you something: they don't respect the Zone and the Zone doesn't like them either, that's why they return home in body bags very often. What else is there? Well, they look down at everyone and act like gods or something. They have no respect for stalker rules and won't ever help you even if you're dying right in front of them. They'll gladly shoot you themselves tho', and they don't get reprimanded for that... I've got a few contacts among them...you know, people always need vodka and...umm, magazines, right? Don't annoy them - they'll whack you for nothing.

The Zone... You see, it's not just a circle a few kilometers in diameter with a nuclear power plant in the center. It's something bizarre. Really bizarre. I doubt anyone in the world could give you a straight answer. I feel the Zone isn't even part of this world. It's a different, detached world, weird and dangerous... and attractive, too. And you know why? Because with a little luck anyone can get rich quick. And the closer you come to the center of the Zone, the higher you will climb on the money ladder.
What's so dangerous about this "different world"?

Hrm... Not a whole lot, except you can croak any second and not know what hit you. A dumb soldier's bullet is the least of your problems, although they've pulled in a lot of military here. Getting shot is basically the most painless kind of death in the Zone. Anomalies, now that's something for you... To die in an anomaly is a terrible thing indeed. Even mutants try to keep away from them. By the way, mutants, these "children of the Zone", are another kind of trouble, so don't go anywhere unarmed. And anyway, to learn about the other dangers, just ask other stalkers.
How does one get rich around here?

Gee, man, what do you think? You don't remember?! Artifacts, that's the most important thing here. All sorts of weird toys that come from the Zone are worth a fortune in the Big Land. Artifacts are more precious than gold, diamonds or pure plutonium. Why? Because no jewelry in the world can give you the supernatural powers that the artifacts possess. Just imagine: you can hang a little gadget on your belt that makes bullets swerve by and never hit you! What else? Well, you can find bits and pieces even right here. For the most valuable pieces though, you'll have to get closer to the center.
Does anyone know what's happening in the center of the Zone?

It's a different tale every time you hear it. It's a bunch of fairy tales... except I don't believe all that otherworldly nonsense. The one thing I do believe is that the center of the Zone contains a lot of valuable artifacts. Only I haven't met anyone who went there and managed to come back alive.
Do you know what happened to me?

What kind of question is that? You see, that's some shady business... They found you in a "death truck", that's a... a kind of corpse carrier, right? These trucks come in from the center of the Zone once in a while, loaded with bodies. No one really knows where they are heading. So, one of them crashed into something and you were found nearby. This is the most amazing thing of all. We've found broken-down corpse carriers before, but no one ever came out of them alive. I'm sorry, bro, that's all I can help you with. The rest you'll have to figure out for yourself.

Yeah, I've heard of a stalker by that name. Rumors is he's one of the few who went to the center of the Zone and made it back. Still, we got so many of these legendary stories about stalker heroes that I can't really guarantee anything. At any rate, I've never dealt with him personally. It seems he has his own trading channels. Anyhow, just ask the folks here in the Zone and you'll be sure to learn a lot more.
Heh, that's another local legend. We haven't had any news from him in a long while. I suppose he perished somewhere deep in the Zone.
What's going on in this neighborhood?

Huh! This is the entrance to the Zone, a sort of an antechamber. We've got an army cordon here. It's relatively quiet, with the exception of a couple of army guys who like to have some fun, go on shakedown runs. Experienced stalkers don't come by very often, so it's mostly rookies. Some settle here for good, yeah... A little ways from here there's a farm made into a rookie camp. Watch yourself though, they aren't afraid to pull the trigger when push comes to shove. What else? You won't make huge profit here, that's for sure. There's a deserted factory across the road. To the North, behind the railroad embankment there are the ruins of a farm. An army outpost lies to the South of here. And beware: the military left some mines in the ground. Also, there used to be a bridge in the center, but it went down somehow. That's about it, really.
Tell me more about the army outpost.

The few soldiers there have been ordered to shoot on sight, so you'd better walk around it and try not to show your ass. If you piss them off, they may call for the Spetsnaz from the further cordon. Those guys can brew up such shit that I won't have any customers for a month... I know the commander a little: he likes to take a swig as much as the next man. I'm surviving on here thanks to him.
I want to know more about the fallen bridge.

That's just a couple of minutes North of here, you'll see it right away. There used to be a bridge over the road, but then it collapsed during one of the blowouts. The army guys built a checkpoint there and don't let anyone through... but if you grease the right palm, you'll pass with no problems. In general, you don't want to show your face there too much, unless you're badly wounded or got a heavy load on you.
Got some more info about the factory?

Cross the road and you'll see a brick building - that's it. Everything worth stealing has already been stolen, just this one box is left. This is the bandits' favorite place. I don't know if they are there now, but watch out just in case. You got nothing valuable on you to take, but do you really wanna go through all that?
You're getting boring, you know that? It's just a farm like any other. The place used to thrive before democracy broke out.
I'd like to find out more.

Huh! This is the entrance to the Zone, a sort of an antechamber. We've got an army cordon here. It's relatively quiet, with the exception of a couple of army guys who like to have some fun, go on shakedown runs. Experienced stalkers don't come by very often, so it's mostly rookies. Some settle here for good, yeah... A little ways from here there's a farm made into a rookie camp. Watch yourself though, they aren't afraid to pull the trigger when push comes to shove. What else? You won't make huge profit here, that's for sure. There's a deserted factory across the road. To the North, behind the railroad embankment there are the ruins of a farm. An army outpost lies to the South of here. And beware: the military left some mines in the ground. Also, there used to be a bridge in the center, but it went down somehow. That's about it, really.
Tell me more about the army outpost.

The few soldiers there have been ordered to shoot on sight, so you'd better walk around it and try not to show your ass. If you piss them off, they may call for the Spetsnaz from the further cordon. Those guys can brew up such shit that I won't have any customers for a month... I know the commander a little: he likes to take a swig as much as the next man. I'm surviving on here thanks to him.
I want to know more about the fallen bridge.

That's just a couple of minutes North of here - you'll see it right away. There used to be a bridge over the road, but then it collapsed during one of the blowouts. The army guys built a checkpoint there and don't let anyone through... but if you grease the right palm, you'll pass with no problems. In general, you don't want to show your face there too much, unless you're badly wounded or got a heavy load on you.
Got some more info about the factory?

Cross the road and you'll see a brick building - that's it. Everything worth stealing has already been stolen, just this one box is left. This is the bandits' favorite place. I don't know if they are there now, but watch out just in case. You got nothing valuable on you to take, but do you really wanna go through all that?
You're getting boring, you know that? It's just a farm like any other. The place used to thrive before democracy broke out.
I'm ok... bleeding all over though. Gimme a medkit, will ya?
Thanks, friend. A little longer and I'd... I owe you one.

We were coming back from a scouting raid... Came across some gangsters. One of our guys got killed right there. They grabbed Nimble 'cause he made up a nice story about a cache full of artifacts. I played dead and that saved my ass.
I need to see Nimble... I'm going after him. Wanna keep me company?
Oh, man... Right now I'm as good fighter as a pair of old socks. I gotta get some of my strength back and return to the camp. I'll let the people know how much you helped me! Thanks again.
OK, you do that. Get well.
Sorry, man. Ain't got a medkit.
Search the bodies... Our guys must have had some... gotta be there somewhere.
Not too good, but I'll live. Damn, why haven't I saved up enough to buy a bulletproof vest. Takes a fool to go deep inside the Zone without one.
Oh, damn, the pain is too much... I'm dying...
Halt, stalker. You can't go any further!

Because that's our territory - Duty territory; and we're very picky about who we let through into our base. If you don't have anything important to discuss with our leaders you ain't getting past here.
I need to pass, I have some business with the Barkeep.
Yes, Barkeep told us about you. You can pass.
Good! You can go, but you better have some really important business with the Barkeep, you hear? And be careful - there's lots of anomalies ahead.
Nothing much. There is a herd of boars wandering nearby that needs to be killed. Take 'em out and consider your passage paid for.
Well, then you better start looking for it!
Well, you can't go through to the bar then.
Thanks for helping us fighting off the boars, take the deserved reward.
So, you killed those boars yet?
Shame. When you start cutting them up for chops, bring me one, would you?
OK, go on. And say "hi" to the Barkeep for me, will you?
Say "hi" for the Barkeep from me when you see him. Oh, and don't forget - there are lots of anomalies on the way, so be careful.
Thanks man- you really helped me out. I won't forget it.

We're trying to clear the Garbage of bandits. My friends are going to ambush a bandit squad nearby. If we don't stop them, they'll take control of the Garbage and nobody will be able to go deeper into the Zone. Talk to our commander. He and his men are near here, at the machinery scrapyard. I'm going there myself - we can go together if you want.
Can you tell me anything about the Garbage?

There ain't much to tell, man. The Garbage is...a dump. Duty fighters guard the northern road to the Bar. You might want to avoid them - Duty is full of real nasty types. To the right are the paths into the Dark Valley but the radiation levels are very high there. A little closer is the Grimpen Mire - terrible marshes full of anomalies. They say it's easy to find some decent artifacts there but even easier to find a lot of trouble. Oh well, at least there ain't no Hound of the Baskervilles in the Mire!

To the right, by the marshes, there's a secret hiding place. You'll see a metallic construction overhanging a puddle. My stash is right below it...I put it there a while ago but you saved my life and you probably need it more than I do anyway. So feel free to use it

Hey stalker, you're just in time! We could really use your help. Those bandit bastards are trying to take control of the Garbage. We had a shootout here half an hour ago and managed to take down three of the dogs. The others retreated, but I'm dead certain they are going to come back with reinforcements any minute now. There aren't too many of us left and another man wouldn't hurt.
We've set up an ambush for them here. They'll be coming from the West, from the Ahroprom Research Institute. Help us destroy their reinforcements and you won't regret it!
It's none of my business and I don't want to get involved.
As you wish. You'll be cursing your stupidity every time you pay those boneheads for passage into the Zone.
You're the man! But I'll play it safe just in case.
We have nothing to talk about.
Thanks man, you really helped us out there. Will you accept some cash? A small token of our gratitude.
Here, this should pay for the medical treatment to recover your health.

Bes's my name. I came to these parts three years ago. Like many of the guys in the Zone I'm a fugitive, wanted for two murders and GBH. A few years ago a friend of mine got battered pretty bad - they had to literally stitch him back together at the hospital. So I paid the bastards who did it a little "visit". When the coppers showed up I had to shoot one of them and jump out of a third-floor window - I've been here since. I've done well in this place but the list of my crimes has got a little longer too. I try to fight the lawless just as I did on the outside. I train up rookies and teach them to survive in the Zone.
So why don't you join Duty if you feel that way?
Duty fights against the Zone, I fight for the people. Besides, they're too closely linked with the government and the army. I don't want to wake up in a cold cell one day.

The Garbage. We are right at home here. You see the heaps of crap? You don't want to go there, trust me. So full of radioactive waste, the Geiger counters go off the scale. This is the only decent way into the Zone and bandits have been increasingly active here lately. They're shit scared of going deeper into the Zone, into Duty territory, like experienced stalkers. But those hyenas just love robbing the rookies here. So, if nobody else cares, we have to stand our ground and fight ourselves.
Everywhere, and especially here, you gotta act like a human being, not like some greed-driven animal. That's my outlook on life.
Are there any large buildings in the Garbage?
Thanks, I don't need anything from you.
I know quite a lot, I could share some information with you.
The job ain't done. Let's finish it and talk later.
Dammit it man, this ain't no time for talking! In a few seconds the only things talking will be guns. Help me fight them off and we can talk after.
Thanks for the help, things got pretty hairy. What are you doing here anyway?
I am looking for information about Strelok. Fox told me about you, so I came for a chat.

OK, you helped me out for free so I'll tell you. One of the guys, a former "digger" called Mole is waiting for me. He dug up Strelok's stash but I don't know if he found anything there. Here are the coordinates of our meeting place. Tell him I sent you. They'll be looking for me now, so I am going to disappear for a while. Here's some cash for your help too.
I am looking for information about Strelok, maybe you know something?

OK, you helped me out for free, so I'll tell you. One of the guys, a former "digger" called Mole, is waiting for me. He dug up Strelok's stash and found documents that explain a few things. He had some bandits on his tail so he had to leave the documents but he remembered the place. They'll be looking for me now so I am gonna disappear for a while. Here are the coordinates of the meeting place with Mole. Tell him I sent you. I'll upload some info to your PDA. Here's some cash for your help too.
Hey man! Things got pretty hairy here a minute ago, I have to say. What are you doing here anyway?
A safe path, stalker. A safe path past the anomalies and straight to the artifacts.
What can one find at the Garbage?

You can find a lot of trouble real quick, that's for sure. But since you're such an explorer I'll tell you what I know. To the East there is a little forest. Nothing interesting there and it's full of dogs, so be careful. This railway track will lead you to a tunnel. There are lots of anomalies there, maybe something else too but I don't know - never really looked very hard. You probably know about the Duty checkpoint in the North. That's basically all I know about this place.
Listen man, what can I find at this Garbage?

You can find a lot of trouble real quick, that's for sure. But since you're such an explorer I'll tell you what I know. To the East there is a little forest. Nothing interesting there and it's full of dogs, so be careful. This railway track will lead you to a tunnel. There are lots of anomalies there, maybe something else too but I don't know - never really looked very hard. You probably know about the Duty checkpoint in the North. That's basically all I know about this place.
Thanks for the info. I'll keep it in mind.
Can you tell me anything about this place?

Well, listen. Everywhere you look it's a big stinking dump. After the accident in 1986 they brought radioactive waste, cranes and all the destroyed machinery here. Basically, it's full of useless crap and a few anomalies here and there. You can even find some artifacts, but they're not spectacular so that's why you'll mostly find rookies in this place. Lots of rookies means lots of bandits, so it's best not to hang around too long. Further to the East there are loads of radiation pockets; you can't go there without protection. Going South you'll reach the border of the Zone. Go North and you'll eventually reach Rostok factory and the stalker bar. To the East is the Dark Valley, a gloomy, misty place. To the West is the Ahroprom Research Institute. I'll mark safe pathways on your map.

In the center there's a large hangar, usually it's pretty quiet there. Worst comes to worst, you can spend the night there. If you go west, in the direction of the Ahroprom, there's a bandit camp there, and that's where those freaks that we killed off a while ago were coming from. But I have a feeling that there's more of them there.
Hmm. What kind of mission? Are you looking for something? Or have you found it already? I've heard there's lots of goodies in here...
I'm sorry, I can't tell you and I don't have the time anyway.
Wait up, don't run yet. You'll have enough opportunities for that... What did you find? Myself, I'm not hanging around here for nothing, you know.
What I found is my business.
I found some documents from X18. The Barkeep promised to pay me well for them.
I don't even know how I wound up here. I'd love to be off as soon as possible.
You haven't found anything worthwhile here, have you? They say there's some information about scientific experiments. They're worth some good money...
Yeah?.. And what if you think really well about it? Are you hiding something from me?
What kind of question is that?
To hell with all that information. I'm barely staying alive in here.
Alright then, off you go. If you find something - I'll be around...

Listen, pal, I've made it through fire, anomalies and monsters in this stinking Zone and as you can see I'm still alive! So don't you tell me what I can or cannot say. I've come here for the documents and I won't leave without them. If they're on you, I advise you to hand them over or rest in peace in this hole! Got it?

If you're lying to me, you're in deep shit. If you've are thinking of screwing me, you're in deep shit, and if you try to shove me some crap, you're in deep shit. In fact, you're so deep in shit that you'll have to swim very hard to keep afloat! Now, let me try one more time for the idiots in the assistance: have you found the documents?
Alrighty, and next time, watch your mouth. You may go now. In case you find something, I'll be around...
This conversation is over. Get out of my way.

That's much better, chum. Now give me all the information you've got, every little thing to the last scrap of paper. And don't even think of pulling anything funny 'cause that joke would be your last...
I'm telling you, stop! I'm not roaming around this rat hole for nothing. Stop horsing around and tell me what you've found. We could even split it...
Uh-huh, sod off now... I got one last advice for you - watch your step, stalker, you'll need it!
OK, OK, cool it... Soon as I get curious, you're grabbing your gun! No more questions. Just be careful, that's all.
Documents, huh... Just so you know, that's exactly what I came here for. And now you will hand them over and get on your way. Understood?
Look, man, stop screwing with my brain. Let's make a deal. I'll give you a nice chunk of money - you won't be sorry.
Listen, my life depends on these documents. Let's see what you got - I think we can come to an agreement...
It's so dark here, I can't see a damn thing. Burers all around... Why did I ever agree to do this?! What about you, how did you wind up here?

I hate it when someone interrupts when I speak. Anyway, I came here for some important documents. If you've found something, spill it out or you'll be in trouble. If you haven't found anything, get lost and stop wasting my time.
I hate it when someone interrupts when I speak. Gimme those documents if you don't want to part with your life.
I'm looking for the leader of Freedom. I've got extremely important information for him.
Oh, and just how important is it?
I came across a group of Duty members not far from your base.

Uh-oh... Looks like Duty is trying to equalize again... Ok, you can enter, but make sure to put that piece away. Don't even think of making a mess in here, or you'll be leaving soon with your feet foremost.
Hey, man! Good to see ya!
To be honest, I can't seem to recall who you are.
Hrm... Yeah, it's fine, no problem.
This place... a nice little place... It's just a normal place, you know... just some people making a living, hehe...
Pfft... You're asking some tough questions, Mr. Stranger!
And what if you cut to the chase?
Hey, relax! Hold on... well... it's just a normal place! A quiet one. All good all around. Yeah...you relax, alight? Don't get all worked up...

Haha, that's a good one! Me... What about me? I'm fine... Well, I... I make food... So, there. I'm a chef! The master of slices and spices! You know - parsley, dill, herbs, stuff like that... Bread, too... Right?
Also, tinned foods... condensed milk with sugar... ham in a can... roast beef with tomato dressing... yeah...
French bread... ketchup... eggs... brown bread... cheese, what have you. Wieners, too! And sausages! Fowl... cold, not flying.
OK, fine, I understand! Tell me your name at least.

Huh?.. Oh! Slavik is my name... that's just a label... No! It's an apron! I've got this white apron... Hey, look... Sit down, don't be a target... 'Cause, that sniper, you know... pick you off! .. They're, you know... They're like that...
Ahh... There is something. Some food there... condensed milk for one...
Eh? Wait. Let me think... Nah, look, do you really need all that? Relax... Sit... Let's sit and have a smoke, OK?
I need to get to the village.
Alright... we just sit here, have a smoke.. Then we go... I'll take you there. No problem.
Don't go to all that trouble. Just tell me how to get there.
Sure... Hey, you're really challenging me here! We'll go there together in just a sec. First we just sit down, OK, and...
Listen, would you know where I can get some equipment?
Equipment? That's some code-speak to me, bro!
Weapons... no... my knife... Want my knife? No? Yeah... Artifacts. What the hell for? Take it easy. C'mon, sit. Light one up with me. Let's talk... about artifacts...
Where can I find artifacts in this area?

That's easy! Artifacts... We got artifacts up to our ears! You know the radioactive swamp? No? Doesn’t matter. It's there. I mean, the artifacts. Are there. You just stop flapping about. Take it easy. Eh? Artifacts... he-he-he... artifacts-shmartifacts...
Sure... sit down though, you're already here.
Eh? Sure thing... relax... OK? Close the door behind me, I'm leaving... ta-da-da-da...
Come, sit down! We'll have a good time...
Let's have a drink, why don't we?
Yes, that was a good one! Hold the bottle... there's some more left.
Ahhhh... awesome! Too bad the bottle's been taken care off already. Got some more?
Yeah, here! Hold your shot steady.
He-he... that was neat! A new one right out of nowhere. You got a warehouse full of them or what?
You, umm... Go ahead, drink. I haven't had much practice lately.
To our victory! Yeaaahh... Ahhh, good times, good times!
C'mon, c'mon, let's do another one... You got an... an-n-nother one, huh?
OK... this will be the last one, alright?... That's all I can handle tonight...
Whoa... look at you... Did you drink all of that on your own?...
Hic! Tha-a-at, bro, is the bbb... bottom! Mmmm... Hhhhhm...
I heard something about Freedom having a Bulldog 6. Sure would be fun to take a look at that baby.
What are you getting at, man? You want me to... to give you... to spill the guts?.. What kind of fool do you take me for?
Calm down. I didn't know it was that serious.
You don't go snooping around for our weapons, got that? Who the hell are you, anyway? I've never seen you before!
Hey, come on! I only asked a question...
Tsss! Be careful with it, alright?
I, umm... ahem... I heard you guys got a Bulldog 6... All I wanna do is just take a look at it, huh? Just get a glimpse of it... what do you say? Just a real quick look...
Whaat?... You, umm... go to... ah, hell, what was it... I forgot! What were you asking about?
Grrr...great... grant... damn! Gre-nade laun-cher... I'd like to... see it!
Nah... that you can't... anything you want, friend, but not that... No can do!
Umm, ok... I, umm... didn't mean anything by it! Just asking...
W-what?.. Oh! Well, it... it's in the village! We actually had... two of 'em... but those... r-retards! They lost it... wasted it!
Hello, brother. I'm a Duty member and we're here on a mission. Would you like to join us?
Then get our of way way... loner.

Excellent. There, behind that hill, is the main breeding ground of those bastards - the "Free" ones - and me and my boys are going to stir it up properly. There's just one little problem. The base has only one entrance and it is well guarded. We thought of busting a hole in the fence, but their sniper in the tower won't let us. I don't want to send my guys to catch his bullets... but you - he'll let you come close and definitely won't shoot on sight. If you take him out, we'll owe you. Plus, if it goes as planned, you'll be able to take any equipment at the base, whatever you want.
I've already told you: all you need to do is knock off the sniper at that tower above the fence. So, what do you say?
Then get out of the way.
That's great! As soon as you take him out, we'll start the fireworks.
There's one small problem. Since the base has only one entrance, a well-guarded one at that, we quickly thought of making another one, but...
I sent my guy at night to blow a hole in the fence. Well, either their sniper is too good or it just wasn't our day, but my guy went down. Now the dynamite is on the fence, but we can't blow it up.
I don't want to send my boys under that asshole's bullets, but you stand a great chance of taking him out. He won't shoot you on sight for sure. What do you say?

Now that's talking business. 2000 from us for the sniper, plus a whole lot of gear at the base. Lots of a dead bodies there, I can promise you that. So, you can help us and help yourself. How about it?
First I have to see how it all looks, then I'll tell you.
We'll wait. But not for long.
Thanks for your help, Marked One. Here is your reward.
We made a deal, didn't we?
So, you knocked off the sniper. Here's your fair compensation.
Step aside, will you? We need to get ready...

We're about to blow up the fence and get the party going. I can see you're a good shooter - maybe you could take care of the tower snipers? With snipers out of the way, we'll have an easier time getting to that spaz Lukash.
Did you knock off the sniper?
Great work! Cleared the road for us... Here's a little something for your troubles.
What do I have to do?
Take care of the other snipers while we deal with these bastards. I can see you're good at that kind of thing.
Well, judge for yourself. I won't make you.
Three assholes down, just like that! You came just in time: didn't blow our cover and came out safe and sound. By the way, I think no one has to know about this, eh?
Perfect. Are you looking for a way to earn some dough, stalker?
What, you don't feel like living anymore?
Then follow us, talk to our commander.
Sure. Skull will tell you everything himself.

What's up, stalker! You're just in time. We're defending against Monolith jerks, and the next wave is about to arrive! Believe it or not, we just had our last sniper knocked off! Can you help? We'll pay back after the fight - you won't be sorry!
That's too bad. Come back if you change your mind.
Great! Go to the tower, there's a sniper rifle and a bunch of ammo. Pick them off from above and don't come down, or you run the risk of getting it from our own guys. Is the assignment clear?

As it should be. I hope to talk to you in the morning. Oh, and don't walk off too far... Or I might think you've decided to run off. And that would mean you-know-what, so keep that in mind! Now, assume your position.
That's right. OK, let's get to work. A Monolith group might be trying to break through here. That means it's gonna get hot real soon.
You're right. I hope they won't be too many...
Go to the tower, there's a Dragunov and some ammo. You'll be able to pick them off from above, but don't come down - watch out for our own bullets. Is the assignment clear?
Stake out your own spot 'cause later you won't have time!
Looks like we took them all down. I say that's enough for today. Thanks for your help! Here, take this - a little bit of money to cheer you up.
Hey, Marked One! Did Lukash send you?
Yes. He asked me to help you.

You're just in time. We're fending off the Monolith jerks, and the next wave is almost here! Believe it or not, we just had our last sniper knocked off! Can you help? We'll pay back after the fight - you won't be sorry!
Looks like we took them all down. I say that's enough for today. Thanks for your help! Here, take this - a little bit of money to cheer you up.
We call it the Barrier... that's what it basi?ally is. It's about the last normal outpost this side of the Zone's center. It's a sort of buffer, you know, between the people and the Wild Territory.

Only death... or serving Monolith. "Our Monolith! In your name we..." Damn! In short, no one needs to go there. That's why we're based here, trying to prevent the muck from infesting the Zone. Like some kind of Jedis, hehe. We also detain the really stupid ones - or at least try not to let them creep into the center.

Who else but us? Duty? They are the shit when it comes to eradicating monsters and eliminating unwanted stalkers, but you should try some time to make them actually do something useful! Do you see them here? No? That's what I mean. We, on the other hand, do not aim to destroy the creatures that live here. A barrier, a normal barrier - that should be enough. As long as no side crosses the line...
Anything can happen. Anything. I don't want to...
Halt! Access to the arsenal is restricted to Freedom members only.
There, now you can enter the arsenal. Except there's a little problem...
Don't sweat it, it's nothing terrible. The lock is jammed, that's all. If you really can't wait, just shoot it to pieces, I'll replace it later.
Skinflint sent me here to clear off the bloodsuckers.
So, stalker, what brought you to this sinister place? My advice is run for your life before it's too late!

Ahh, now that's another matter... Hell knows what's going on here! A couple of our folks went into the village and never came back. Heck, we're not keen on dying yet! We're sitting here, deciding what to do next.
Hey, bro, way to freak out last night!

We were just sitting around and then you barge in piss drunk! And you barge right into the village, right into those bloodsuckers! Good thing we were there to put some brakes on you and make you sleep it off.
And what are you doing here?
Hell knows what's going on here! A couple of our folks went into the village and never came back. Heck, we're not keen on dying yet! We're sitting here, deciding what to do next.
Wow! You rock! Heck, you came out alive, so the next round of drinks is on you!

No. Our guys were supposed to go to the old water tower; that's probably where they went down. Well, if you made a deal with Skinflint, go there and check it out... Write down an address, will you? We'll write to your family if something goes wrong.
Why are you wandering around like a ghost?
Seems to me you're too kind... OK, I'll go.
Was this the best place you could find? This is probably the shittiest spot in the whole Zone...
What's so bad about this place?

Hell knows what's going on here! Looks like the village is now home to a swarm of bloodsuckers. A couple of our folks went into the village and never came back. Heck, we're not keen on dying yet! We're sitting here, deciding what to do next.

Right on! If you have nothing else to do and your head isn't working right, you're welcome to take a stroll through the village. If you clear off those critters, Skinflint will surely shell something out for you. Write down an address, will you? We'll write to your family if something goes wrong.
OK, I'll go check it out.
You didn't go through the village, did you?
A couple of our folks went there and didn't come back. Did you happen to see them?
No, I didn't. Just a few corpses on the ground.
Yep. Go ahead and look for yourself, it's safe now - I mowed them down.
Wow! That's a trick! Know what... go see Skinflint at our base. He pays for good work.
OK, I'll go check it out.
Wow, you showed them! Know what... go see Skinflint at our base. He pays for good work.
Was this the best place you could find? This is probably the shittiest spot in the whole Zone...
What's so bad about this place?
Ah, hell knows. Looks like bloodsuckers have settled down in the village. A couple of our folks went into the village and never came back. Heck, we're not keen on dying yet!

Right on! If you have nothing else to do and your head isn't working right, you're welcome to take a stroll through the village. If you clear off those critters, Skinflint will surely shell something out for you. Write down an address, will you? We'll write to your family if something goes wrong.
OK, I'll go check it out.
Wow, that was awesome! My congratulations! And the best thing is, you're still alive...

Since you helped our guys at the Barrier, you can count on getting some rest on our territory, as well as our help. Oh, and my personal appreciation - a bit of legal tender for you - from this moment on, I'm enlisting you in our group.
Hey, stalker. What do you need?
Then let's get out of here, OK? Time is money.
I saw a group of Duty members in a farmstead near your base. About eight men, well equipped.
Why the hell did you split from the Barrier? Get out of here and don't let me catch sight of you near my base ever again.

Damn, these punks just won't settle down! Fine, we'll have to teach them a lesson. Hey, stalker, want to help us for a reward? Talk to Max, our chief. You two will get a crew together and root out those suckers.
So, you didn't handle the snitch business and failed at the Barrier, too? Get lost from my base and don't ever show up again!
For real? How many of them did you say there were?

You're a speedy one, stalker... OK, so, do you mind helping us out with something else? I'm ashamed to admit that there's a snitch among us. His name is Pavlik. He's leaking valuable info and that must be stopped.
You must follow him to the farmstead and discover whose rat he is. He doesn't know you and isn't aware of our conversation either, so...
You think I have work for you to do?

We've learned there's a rat in our midst, by the name of Pavlik. He's leaking valuable information. We know that for a fact, but don't have proof. Your task is to spy him out and discover who he is singing for. I think he's going to a meet tonight. You'll follow him quietly. Just be careful that he doesn't see you - he's extremely cautious. After you find out the identity of his connection, bump him off and the other guy, too, for good measure.

We've learned there's a rat in our midst, by the name of Pavlik. He's leaking valuable information. We know that for a fact, but don't have proof. Your task is to spy him out and discover who he is singing for. I think he's going to a meet tonight. You'll follow him quietly. Just be careful that he doesn't see you - he's extremely cautious. After you find out the identity of his connection, bump him off and the other guy, too, for good measure.
There's one thing I wanted to ask you about.
He just left, so you'll have to catch up with him. Remember: you can and should bump him off, but only after he's exposed the agent he's ratting to. You'll have to eliminate the other guy, too.
I told you to go talk to Max.
So, the assignment is complete... Good, good. I've got another one for you!
Sadly, I have to admit there's a snitch in our ranks. His name is Pavlik. He's leaking valuable information, and that must be stopped.

Exactly. And I think he will go to his meet some time between 10 and 11 p.m. It's going to be dark enough, so no one sees him, but the nighttime mutants won't be out yet. Go forth, stalker. After you're done, you will become a member of our group. Remember, you must catch the snitch red-handed! You've got to take him down during his meet, otherwise the mission will have failed.

Great job, Marked One! Here, take this... your payment for taking care of the snitch. You're welcome to rest on our territory whenever you want and you can also count on our help. Only, there's one more business I'd like you to do for me. Are you not too tired?
If you feel up to it, go to the outpost near the Red Forest exit. We've had reports claiming that either mutants or Monolith are planning a new attempt to break through there. The guys need help.
Well, you did help the guys at the Barrier, so - relax.
Relax. There's no work right now.
Yeah, you screwed up that mission... that sucks. Still, there's always another chance!

Certain things point to the fact that very soon Monolith is gonna try to break through from the Red Forest side. You gotta go see Cap - he's the commander at the Barrier - and see if he needs any help. Well, it's pretty obvious he does, really...
No, I won't do it yet.
Think about our guys, they're gonna get wasted! You see, there's no one else but you that I can send... nobody.
You failed the mission, so we might as well kiss those documents good-bye. We have no use for losers like you.
Well, you screwed up that mission... that sucks. Still, you did help the guys at the Barrier; heck - you can relax on our territory. In case something comes up, we'll help you.
I've told you - go to the Barrier.
We'll talk after we take down those Duty assholes.
Lukash said that you'll do. Did you see those... at the farmstead?
Hell... Duty's got something up their ass again! Oh well. Come with us, will you?
Can't say for sure. I don't think there's too many.
About six to eight people. Reasonably equipped.
Yes. Lukash told me to intercept the Duty courier on the way from Red Forest to the Dead City. Could you tell me the best place to do that?

Hmm. Best thing would be to catch him before he reaches the farmstead near the passage to Dead City. Reinforcements are sure to be waiting for him there, so you'd have trouble dealing with them all. Plus, that way you'd have to pass through the village, which is a bad thing. Better go East of the village, and fast.
Believe me - not a single good thing. Lots of monsters roaming about, and anomalies, too. I'll tell you about that later.
I see you're the right kind of guy! Would you mind doing another small job for me? Get it done and I'll give you my Black Kite. A great piece, and the punch it packs!
But what am I supposed to do?

There's this one guy who dug in at the swamp. My boys saw him rushing from the village over hedge and ditch. He must have seen something that knocked a few screws loose in his head. Now he's shooting anyone who comes in sight. You've got to find out what it was he saw, 'cause our people are too afraid to go into the village.
Here's the flash drive I got from the crazy guy.
Hey, thanks, stalker! Take this gun - you've earned it.
Just what's in the magazine. Sorry, that's all I got. Go see Skinflint, he might have some.
Good luck, then. Don't forget - there's an old minefield, but with the help of a few good old bolts you can go around any mine.
So, what did you learn? What happened to that psycho?
Here's a flash drive - I got it from the crazy guy.
What do you want to know?
I've got business to discuss. Can you show me the way to your boss?
You want Lukash - he's our leader. Go to the main building.
I see you managed alright. Give the documents to Lukash.
Go to Lukash and make your report, then we'll talk, alight?
Man, you're in the Freedom base! Relax, make yourself at home!

Behave, OK? Got it? Try fooling around and you'll be sorry. We had a slow-witted one here once; he pissed everyone off so bad that we made him take a hike on the minefield - naked. You should have heard Skinflint cursing when that dude's arm fell square on his head! That was something special... yeah... So, you got the idea?
Ah, a rookie... Come over a sec.
Where are you going? Stay! It's nice here... and we'll be having dinner tonight.
What?! Shit, why didn't you tell me that before! We've just been wasting time. Get lost, buttface. And this time I'm dead serious...
What didn't you get? Oh, you haven't heard the story about the slow-witted ones, huh?
Alright, look. Think about it: these are lean times...
Well, that's easy! A hungry soldier is a bad soldier. And there's so many of us...
Where would we get that much meat? And look, here you are!
You got something that's broken, man?
Ahhh, you're not here to ask for my help? Whew! I'm getting sick and tired of that. Soon as something breaks, they run to me and start whining about it.
I'm a mechanic. A Mr-Fix-It of sorts... I repair guns and whatever else you bring me. Right now, I'm working on these cars.
Pfft! Nothing but rubbish. I'm totally beat. Listen, you got any vodka on you? I'd, umm... you... want a nice scope? The best optics there is!
It's your loss. If you have some - come in, don't be a stranger.
Well, thanks! How nice of you. Now I'll have something to help me relax later on.
No way, did you really bring vodka?
Here's a tricky question - where do I go for things like artifacts and weapons?
You come to me, with money. Or go to the village and get them for free, but the risks are much higher.
The village. It's full of anomalies - rookies stumble on them all the time. Our guys just left to go there, you can join them if you want.
What good things did you bring me?
You should do something useful instead. Specially as I've got something you might be interested in... Want to earn, huh?

I can give you a Crystal if you do me one particular favor. A nasty situation has developed... Lately, no one has been bringing any artifacts from the village - they are too scared. They say bloodsuckers have settled in there. These days, I can't even send anyone - in fact, I'm that close of asking Duty for help!
A couple of guys went to the village and didn't come back, and the other three are just sitting there on the outskirts. If you destroy the bloodsuckers, I'll give you a Crystal. What do you say?
Hey, it's your call... Sooner or later someone will clear them off. Meanwhile, Crystal is rare and rather expensive, too.
Oh, that's swell. If you find more stuff like this, bring it over - I'll pay a good price.
If you help them in their hunt, I'll give you an artifact called Crystal. So, what do you say?
We had a chat and then went on a walk across the village trying to catch some mutant...
And? Cut to the chase already!

We took down some kind of beast, but the guys went down, too. Right as we came to the water tower, they all grabbed their heads, and I had blood in my eyes, too... That shitbucket affects your brain. I killed the thing, but it was too late to help the guys.
That blows. Murk was a skilled stalker. Damn shame. Well, take this artifact for helping the boys.
Well, guys went hunting and I returned here...
Can I stock up on some equipment here?
For a fixed price, anything is possible.
Damn, stalker! You're as explosive as diarrhea! OK, a deal is a deal, here's your Crystal.
So, how did the hunting go?
Hmm, interesting... All kind of guck is spreading from the center of the Zone - but where does it come from?.. OK, take this for your troubles.
But I asked you to zap those bloodsuckers in the village!
No problem. Of course, in that case there will be no reward.
Uh-huh, so, you didn't settle that little problem... Therefore, I'll keep the artifact to myself.
People have been saying you took care of these bloodsuckers in the village. Is that true?
Hmm, interesting... All kind of guck is spreading from the center of the Zone - but where does it come from?.. OK, take this for your troubles.
So, what have you brought with you?
Is there a cook around here?
Sure is, how could we do without one! What the heck do you need him for, anyway?
Well, I need a little bit of food.
He's in his kitchen. Usually, he sits there and mutters.
What did you expect? A drunk is a drunk. If he couldn't cook, we'd get rid of him a long time ago. He's in the kitchen. I'll tell you how to get there.
Lukash is our leader. Go to the main building.
Same to you. What brings you here?
Who's the boss around here? I've got business to discuss with him.
Lukash is our leader, you can find him in the main building. But as you enter, make sure to put your piece away - unless you wanna get shot for nothing.
We had some shit happen - once a killer got this close to dropping Lukash. We stopped taking any risks after that.
So-so. Keep out, don't bother me.

A real radioactive puddle. Better not touch it, or you'll get enough rads to make your navel button unwind. There's a character in the middle of it, doesn't let anyone come close. Well, I can feel for him: who'd want to mix with idiots? 'Cause a normal person would definitely stay away from there.
Hell knows. They say he's one of our folks that's been to the village.
Well, there is one little hamlet nearby. Full of mutants and anomalies. I haven't been there yet - I only came to this base just recently. Ask Max - he's been here a hundred years.
Listen, where can I stock up on some ammo? Rostok is kind of a long walk from here, I don't feel like going there.
Go to the main building and you'll find it on the first floor.
Whassup, rambling man? Looking to drop in at our place, huh?
How about not. I'm here on business.
C'mon, join us. It's a lot of fun.
Battleship sunk. Shit, stop tricking me! Got anything else?
Twenty-one. Damn, got me again! You're a slick one. OK, so you dunno the password, do you?
C'mon, please let me in. I can't spend the night in the fields, can I?
Why not - the weather is fine, almost like summer. Well, alright, if you pass the test - you can go, if not - get lost.
Sure. Three things every real man has to know.
OK, the easiest one first. Ready? Alright, what is Pamela Anderson's bra size?
You're a real idiot. Go fly a kite!
You're a real idiot. Go fly a kite!
Good job! I can tell you're the right kind of man!
A bit more difficult now! OK... What's the round capacity of a cartridge that goes into the Russian submachine gun Bizon?
Awesome! I'm liking you more and more... don't get me wrong, not that way!
On to the third one! What's the flight speed of a South African swallow ?
How am I supposed to know that?! Do you even have the answer?
Of course not. That's the thing, I can't seem to solve that puzzle... Well, OK, you can pass.
OK, wanna try again? Alright. Question: what is Dotty Strangelove's bra size?
You're a pushy sort! I repeat: what's the capacity of the magazine that goes into the Russian SMG Bizon?
Let's move up the street. If anything happens, cover me.

Thanks for the help, Marked One. That was a hard fight. We can rest here. If you wanna keep going, I'll tell you more about the area. If you go back and turn right you'll reach the bus station. That area is open and not very safe. If you go forward you'll end up in front of the hotel. To the right of it is the Monolith base. Those guys have no limits. Behind the base is the stadium. We were planning to get to the station through it. That's all.
Thanks for the help. Good luck.
Where's the money for the mission?
Find my deputy and you'll get your money!
OK but don't lie to me this time!
Leha the Jaw is my name! These rogues are my crew!
My boys and I are resting! When we're done we'll head to Prypiat!
What do you know about Prypiat?
It's an old abandoned city from the communist era! From history! I reckon it's full of monsters and hordes of Monolith fighters now! No one who has gone there before ever returned!
No doubt about it - nobody has ever come back from there.
No, I ain't seen any soldiers for a long time.

Rumour has it that they showed up right by the Red Forest, by the antennae! When they were turned off many people went to the Red Forest and further into Prypiat! We've been here for a day and we haven't seen anyone. Someone probably blocked the road here.
You think the military blocked the road to the Red Forest?

I don't think it was the military, I haven't seen any for a long time but military stalkers could have done it! They don't waste any time. They're the no-nonsense types, they work quickly and efficiently in their teams or solo... it's weird that they didn't block the road here earlier.
Why did they not go to Prypiat or block the road there?
I don't know. Maybe they're afraid or they're getting ready for something. I can only guess.
Maybe you've got a mission for me?
Well, I have one but it's not easy...will you do it?
Saying it's not easy doesn't scare me!
Nah, no way. I don't have the time!
I understand. I'm afraid myself... would prefer to spend some money instead of sending my guys or going myself.

Good! The job is to find my scouts, Crowbar and Log! They went to Prypiat since I decided not to rush in and sent my best guys instead! Find them alive and bring them here. If they're dead bring me their weapons! Do this for me and you'll get 2000!!!
Not enough. How about 4000 and we have a deal?
That's all I can offer you for this job!
OK, I'll wait for you here!
You're trying to pull a fast one on me. He didn't have anything!
I'm gonna waste you right now!
If I see you again you're a dead man!
I can't give you anything, I don't have any money. You can keep his stuff if you want.
I don't have any money! Go away!
Find him fast if you want your money!
I found your boys. They died but here are their weapons!
Thanks, they're dead then. I don't think there's any point going to Prypiat now! There's a problem though - I don't have your money. My deputy disappeared and he had all the money!
OK, I'll find him! Tell me where to look for him. And don't even think about trying to cheat me out of my cash this time!!!
I don't think he got very far. He's probably here or here, I'll mark it on the map for you! When you find him, bring him or his PDA back here!
Find them fast! I can't wait forever!!! Good luck!

Thank you... err... Marked One, right? You helped save our associate Kruglov and in order to show my sincere appreciation I would like to present you with one of our working suits. It comes in very handy, you should know that... Could I be of additional help to you?
I'm looking for Lab X16. The Barkeep told me you might have info about its location.

We've long had an interest in Lab X16...or, rather, in the mechanism inside it that generates emissions which directly affects people's consciousness. I'm afraid I have to disappoint you: the place is impossible to penetrate without special psi-armor. The outcome is known in advance - even if you could evade the swarms of zombies that infest the laboratory entrance, you run the risk of becoming one of them. This is all very serious. However, we've designed a screening device to help shield the emission, but to fine-tune it we have to obtain additional measurements. If you help us with this task, we could probably provide you with a fully calibrated prototype for field testing.

If you, together with our associate, take all the necessary measurements, I will prepare a fully workable prototype of the armor and will give it to you. It will most likely let you get inside the laboratory.
Excellent! Talk to Kruglov and get going. He is already equipped with the necessary gear.
Excellent! Talk to Semenov and get going. He's got everything you will need.
No.. right now I don't want to sign up for this.
That's too bad, a real pity... I don't know how or when we could carry out this measurement expedition on our own.
Yes, Sir? What might you be looking for?
I'm looking for Lab X16. The Barkeep told me you might have info about its location.
Thank you, Marked One, for rescuing Kruglov. Take this pressurized suit as a reward for your troubles. Also, I suspect you didn't come here for a simple reward, am I right?
I'm looking for Lab X16. The Barkeep told me that you might have info about its location.

We have been studying the emission in Lab X16 for a long time, but you won't get in there without special equipment. We've developed a psi-protection device that can help shield you from the emission, but needs some fine-tuning. If you could help us with that, I could give you a working prototype that you can use to get inside the laboratory.
Excellent, talk to Kruglov and get going. He's got everything you'll need.
No, I'm not up for this yet.
That's a real pity. So far we've been unable to send a measurement expedition of our own.
Yes, Sir? What might you be looking for?

We've long had an interest in Lab X16...or, rather, in the mechanism inside it that generates emissions which directly affects people's consciousness. I'm afraid I have to disappoint you: the place is impossible to penetrate without special psi-armor. The outcome is known in advance - even if you could evade the swarms of zombies that infest the laboratory entrance, you run the risk of becoming one of them. This is all very serious. However, we've designed a screening device to help shield the emission, but to fine-tune it we have to obtain additional measurements. If you help us with this task, we could probably provide you with a fully calibrated prototype for field testing.

We have been studying the emission in Lab X16 for a long time, but you won't get in there without special equipment. We've developed a psi-protection device that can help shield you from the emission, but needs some fine-tuning. If you could help us with that, I could give you a working prototype that you can use to get inside the laboratory.
Excellent! Talk to Kruglov and get going. He is already equipped with the necessary gear.
Excellent! Talk to Semenov and get going. He's got everything you will need.
You don't understand, there's an emitter. No one has been able to come closer than half a kilometer - it makes a zombie out of anyone. Going there without a suit is just like suicide, get it?
To hell with it, just tell me all you know.

OK, but don't say I didn't warn you. Vasiliev worked on the laboratory problem. About a week ago, he and stalker by the name of Ghost attempted to get inside the laboratory. After several hours we felt a shake and emissions stopped; after another hour, we got a signal from Vasiliev, it was coming from the swamp but then vanished. By the looks of things, Vasiliev is gone. Try to find his body: he had accurate information about the laboratory - where it is and how to get in. He might have also known how to turn off the emissions. The laboratory entrance is most likely hidden in the basement of one of the buildings in the power plant area.
No.. right now I don't want to sign up for this.
That's too bad, a real pity... I don't know how or when we could carry out this measurement expedition on our own.

Thank you... mmm... Marked One, right? You helped save our associate Kruglov and in order to show my sincere appreciation I would like to present you with one of our working suits. It comes in very handy, you should know that...

You did a splendid job, Marked One! Now that the emissions are turned off, we will be able to order new expeditions and explore previously inaccessible territories. And also... from now on we will conduct a most favorable pricing policy toward you.
Yes, Marked One? What are you looking for?
You did an excellent job. If the measurements are correct, the prototype should provide you with maximum protection from emission.

Now your path to the laboratory is clear... except that if the source of the emission isn't destroyed and doesn't lose its power, you may not have enough time to get back. So, my dear Sir, here is the deal. Our calculations have produced an estimated location of the source as well as the fact that it is under ground. With your permission, I have already marked the spot on your map.
How exactly does one get inside Lab X16? Where is the entrance?

Vasiliev was working on the lab issue. About a week ago he and a stalker called Ghost tried to get inside.
